name: Apple Developer Documentation: HealthKit
why: Defines HealthKit as the central health and fitness data repository on iPhone and Apple Watch, gated by user permission.

name: Apple Developer Documentation: Protecting user privacy
why: Canonical source for explicit per-type authorization and privacy messaging.

name: Apple Support: View your data in Health on iPhone
why: Documents Summary, Highlights, Trends, and drill-down behavior in the Health app.

name: Apple Watch - Close Your Rings
why: Defines the public meaning of Move, Exercise, and Stand rings.

name: Apple Support: Track daily activity with Apple Watch
why: Explains the daily ring habit model: sit less, move more, get exercise, close rings.

name: Apple Support: View activity, workouts, trends, and awards in Fitness
why: Documents Summary, activity rings, trends, workouts, meditations, and awards structure.

aspect: HealthKit HIG and Apple Health badge
name: Apple Human Interface Guidelines: HealthKit
why: Guidance for HealthKit-related UI, Apple Health icon usage, terminology, and privacy.

name: Apple Licensing and Trademarks: Works with Apple Health
why: Rules for badge use, subordinate placement, social media restrictions, and terminology.

name: Apple Human Interface Guidelines: Color
why: System colors adapt across appearance modes and accessibility settings.

name: Apple Human Interface Guidelines: Typography
why: System type, legibility, hierarchy, and Dynamic Type guidance.

name: SF Symbols
why: Native symbol system aligned with San Francisco; use through platform APIs, do not bundle Apple font files.

name: kingstinct/react-native-healthkit
why: Modern, TypeScript-first HealthKit bindings with Promise API, Nitro Modules, hooks, and Expo plugin.

name: agencyenterprise/react-native-health
why: Established React Native HealthKit package with large community footprint and many examples; use as compatibility reference.

name: Haider-Mukhtar/ReactNative-Apple-Health-IOS
why: Expo + TypeScript sample using react-native-health for common metrics and hook-based data fetching.

aspect: Apple Watch bridge from React Native
name: watch-connectivity/react-native-watch-connectivity
why: Best reference for React Native iPhone app communicating with a native Swift/Objective-C Watch app via WatchConnectivity.

name: CRIIPI11/rn-watch-connect
why: Modern alternative for communicating with paired Apple Watch, still assumes the watch app is Swift/SwiftUI.

name: msimms/OpenWorkoutTracker
why: Native iOS/watchOS workout tracker; useful for watch-specific data flow, workout session architecture, and SwiftUI patterns.

name: skulptapp/skulpt
why: Modern local-first workout tracker using React Native, Expo, SQLite, HealthKit, Health Connect, Apple Watch, and Live Activity support.

name: RuochenLyu/apple-health-analyst
why: Privacy-first Apple Health analyzer emphasizing cross-metric insights, trends, reports, and agent-driven health analysis.

name: Janjiran/react-native-workouts
why: Experimental Expo module for Apple WorkoutKit to create, preview, and sync workouts to Apple Watch/Fitness app.

name: thecodehunter/react-native-activity-rings
why: React Native activity ring component useful for API shape and theming ideas; not enough for full Apple-style >100% ring behavior.

aspect: Charts for React Native health UI
name: FormidableLabs/victory-native-xl
why: High-performance React Native charting powered by D3, Skia, and Reanimated; strong fit for trends and health metric charts.

name: Shopify React Native Skia
why: 2D graphics primitives for custom arcs, gradients, shaders, chart overlays, and animated ring details.

name: React Native Reanimated
why: Modern animation engine for RN; use for ring interpolation, number rolls, card expansion, and gesture-linked motion.

name: Expo Router Native Tabs
why: Native tab bars on Android and iOS; useful for iOS-feeling tab navigation while keeping RN app structure.

name: matinzd/react-native-health-connect
why: React Native wrapper around Android Health Connect, the Android equivalent integration path for health data.

name: the-momentum/open-wearables
why: Self-hosted unified wearable API reference for normalizing multiple providers beyond HealthKit and Health Connect.

name: JanSzewczyk/workout-tracker
why: React Native/Expo workout app with dashboard, progress, achievements, offline/private positioning; useful for gamification UI.
